Off Calendar Oct-18-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MATTHEW PARDO, Esq. of Strategic Legal Practices (Tel: 310-929-4900) for plaintiff;
Atty. LESLIE N. MORALES, Esq. of Bowman and Brooke LLP (Tel: 310-768-3068) for defendant Kia Motors America, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KIA MOTORS AMERICA INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjucation.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Kia Motors America, Inc.'s ("Kia") motion for summary judgment is denied. Kia's alternative motion for summary adjudication is also denied.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Set For Trial On Sep-28-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chrstine Haw, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Patrick Raue, Esq. 619-376-2526 of Bowman and Brooke LLP for Defendant Kia Motors through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues the fifth cause of action for breach of implied warranty of merchantability on the Demurrer.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT KIA MOTORS AMERICA INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Kia Motors America, Inc.'s demurrer is overruled as to the third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

The demurrer as to the third cause of action is overruled. Liberally construing the allegations, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a violation of Civil Code 1793.2(a).

The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of written express warranty is overruled. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to establish a claim. (See Compl. 10-11, 46-48.)

The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for breach of implied warranty of merchantability is overruled. (see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

One order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chrstine Haw, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Patrick Raue, Esq. 619-376-2526 of Bowman and Brooke LLP for Defendant Kia Motors through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues fraud claim and prayer for punitive damages on the mtoion to strike.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT KIA MOTORS AMERICA INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Kia Motors America, Inc.'s motion to strike plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages is granted. Punitive damages are not recoverable on Plaintiff's sixth cause of action for fraud, as the Court has sustained Defendant's demurrer to that cause of action without leave to amend. Moreover, the Song-Beverly Act does not authorize a court to award punitive damages. (See Civ. Code 1794.) None of the cases cited by Plaintiff, all of which addressed the distinct civil penalty provision of the Act (id. 1794(c)), holds to the contrary.

Defendant's motion to strike Plaintiff's prayer for civil penalties for a violation of Civil Code 1793.2(a)(3) (Compl. 98) is denied. The Song-Beverly Act expressly authorizes a civil penalty for a willful failure to comply with "any obligation under this chapter or under an implied or express warranty or service contract." (Civ. Code 1794(c); see Schreidel v. American Honda Motor Co. (1995) 34 Cal.App.4th 1242, 1249-1250.) Defendant ignores this provision. Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant willfully failed to comply with its statutory obligations.

Defendant's motion to strike Plaintiff's prayer for prejudgment interest is denied. Defendant fails to support that motion with any authority or argument, and it raises a factual issue (whether plaintiff's damages are reasonably capable of being made certain) that cannot be determined on the face of the complaint.

An order is signed for both motions. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
